_meta.capability: v3.10.0 R6a — the `security-hunter` adversarial-reviewer shape (6th shape). It is NOT a Layer 3 `verify_*` severity; it is a code-review finding routed as a solution requirement with `origin.kind: "security-finding"`. This fixture is the canonical well-formed routed-SR example the doc catalog references.
_meta.trigger: backend-dep task shape AND the diff touches auth/ AND adds a dependency — all three security-hunter triggers fire on this slice
_meta.note: security-finding spawns the owning team DIRECTLY (the finding already names {class, file, line, evidence, remediation}); it does NOT route through diagnostic-research-team (that is for test-failure origins).

security_findings.evidence: GET /api/admin/users added without the require_admin permission dependency its sibling admin routes carry (compare src/api/admin/audit.py:18).
security_findings.remediation: Add the require_admin dependency to the new route so an unprivileged session cannot enumerate users.

security_findings.evidence: SQL built by f-string interpolation of the request query param: f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE name LIKE '%{q}%'" — unparameterized, SQL-injectable.
security_findings.remediation: Use a parameterized query / the ORM's bound parameters; never interpolate request input into SQL.

security_findings.evidence: New dependency `fast-sql-helper==0.3.1` added with no Reuse Decision citation and no stated reason in the change.
security_findings.remediation: Either justify the dependency with a Reuse Decision + reason, or remove it in favour of the stdlib / an already-vendored helper.

routed_solution_requirement.problem_summary: The new admin user-list endpoint can be called without admin privileges and builds its search query by interpolating the raw request param into SQL, and the slice adds an unjustified third-party dependency.
routed_solution_requirement.expected_behavior: GET /api/admin/users requires an admin session, the search query is parameterized, and any added dependency carries a Reuse Decision + justification.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/admin/users with a non-admin session returns 403 (the require_admin dependency is present)
the user-search query is parameterized (no request input interpolated into SQL); an injection probe returns no rows / errors safely
the added dependency is removed OR justified with a Reuse Decision and a stated reason
an integration test covers the 403-without-admin path and the parameterized-query path
routed_solution_requirement.blast_radius: single endpoint + one manifest line; no schema changes
